title: Feed families in Karachi this winter
story: My name is Bilal Ahmed and I coordinate a volunteer food-relief drive out of Masjid Bilal in the Orangi Town neighbourhood of Karachi (Sector 11½, near Banaras Chowk). Since this Ramadan, the sharp rise in flour and cooking-oil prices has pushed many families on our blocks below the line where they can afford even one daily meal. Over the past four months our small group has handed out monthly ration bags — 10 kg flour, 5 kg rice, 3 kg lentils, and 2 litres of cooking oil — to roughly 60 households, at about PKR 4,500 (≈ $16) per bag. We are now serving close to 140 families and can no longer keep up with demand. We are raising $9,000 to fund three months of rations for these 140 households (about 420 bags) plus transport from the Sabzi Mandi wholesale market. Distributions happen every second Saturday after Zuhr in the masjid courtyard; bag counts and hand-over slips are logged by our volunteers Imran, Farah, and Abdul Sattar. Any surplus will carry the drive through the winter months. JazakAllah khayr for your generosity and your du'a.
vouching_narrative: My whole community vouches for me and my work. Everyone in our neighborhood knows who I am and what I do for the people here. I have been serving this community for many years and all the families here will tell you that I am trustworthy and honest. You can ask anyone in our area and they will confirm that I am a good person doing good work. My friends and neighbors have all seen the distributions I do and they all support me fully. The entire community is behind this campaign and everyone here knows me personally. I do not have formal documentation but the trust of my community is the strongest proof there is. My family also vouches for me and they have seen everything I have done. The community elders know me well and they approve of this campaign. Everyone here believes in what I am doing and you should too.
